key_points: Boland Hall fire left permanent, strict fire safety culture campus-wide
No flammable furnishings allowed; every student reminded of 2000 tragedy annually
Dorm tour videos still used as reference guides by incoming students
key_quotes: Every building, not just residence halls, takes fire safety EXTREMELY seriously
You might have left SHU, but my friends and I have been using your dorm tour as a reference guide
The school is also extremely strict on furnishings students bring in (e.g. no flammable lampshades)

key_points: Weekends described as dead with little to do on campus
Listed clubs are often inactive or meet during class hours
Without a car, social life is severely limited for students
key_quotes: The Seton Hall in the brochures and the tours was nothing like the reality with nothing to do on campus on the weekends
Many clubs are listed but are inactive
With nothing to do on campus and no car, it wasn't very enjoyable

key_points: Meal plan structure changed multiple times, confusing returning students
Unlimited meal plans were eventually introduced after years of limited options
Little specific praise or criticism beyond structural changes
key_quotes: When I was a freshman there was no unlimited meal plan, then my sophomore year they were there but there was still other options, now as a senior they're all unlimited like what

key_points: No direct student mentions of mental health resources or counseling
Anxiety mentioned anecdotally by SHU accounting alumni vlogger
Institutional trauma from Boland Hall fire still referenced in orientation
key_quotes: I am also a hugely anxious person, it's so hard right
Going from that horrible graphic video of people dying, immediately into doing new student ice breakers was… a choice

key_points: Scholarships available but unevenly distributed among enrolled students
$750 enrollment deposit is unusually high and typically non-refundable
$100,000 debt for a marketing degree widely condemned by Reddit users
key_quotes: Jesus Christ. $750 for a deposit is crazy
Absolutely no. Do not go 6 figures in on a marketing degree please
I'm currently a student there and I have not received anything near that which would really help considering how expensive the school is

key_points: Accounting program has produced Big Four hires, a concrete positive outcome
Science and business are cited as the stronger departments
One alum called it 'an elevated High School' with limited intellectual rigor
key_quotes: Science and business is their big area
I would not have made that choice if I was given it again. Likewise, I found it to be an elevated High School
I am also pursuing accounting at Seton Hall

key_points: Administration did refund a $750 deposit when emailed, a rare win
Enrollment deposit policy is strict and non-refundable by default
Gap between brochure promises and campus reality repeatedly noted
key_quotes: I EMAILED THEM AND IM GETTING MY DEPOSIT REFUNDED LETS GOOO
Unfortunately, at most schools, admission/enrollment deposits are technically nonrefundable
The Seton Hall in the brochures and the tours was nothing like the reality

key_points: South Orange is a quiet suburban town with some charm
One commenter inaccurately called it 'the ghetto of East Orange'; actual location is South Orange
NYC proximity touted as internship advantage, especially for business students
key_quotes: SETON HALL IS IN THE GHETTO OF EAST ORANGE, NEW JERSEY
The campus, class offerings, as well as the town of South Orange just gets better and better
Going to Seton Hall where I will be able to do internships in New York

key_points: Accounting grads have landed Big Four jobs, a legitimate career pipeline
NYC proximity creates real internship access for business and finance students
Name recognition drops sharply outside the Northeast; largely unknown in places like California
key_quotes: Rutgers has a much better reputation and name recognition especially the further away you go. In California most people have heard of Rutgers, only basketball fans have heard of Seton Hall
I am also pursuing accounting at Seton Hall. Congratulations! Really happy for you
Going to Seton Hall where I will be able to do internships in New York

key_points: $100,000 debt for a marketing degree universally condemned on Reddit
Tuition over $40,000/year with scholarship distribution described as uneven
Rutgers repeatedly recommended as a better-value alternative by students and alumni
key_quotes: NO. New Jersey has many fine state universities. Choose one of them for 1/4 the cost
Just the thought of going to college for $40,000+ in tuition per year really freaks me out
Rutgers is much cheaper than Seton Hall and is a better school

key_points: Campus feel described as quiet, small, and underwhelming for social students
Strong niche communities exist but require active searching to find
Transfers to Rutgers and other schools are a recurring theme in the data
key_quotes: My son just transferred after his first semester
I left Seton Hall to go to Rutgers but that did not pan out well so I went back to SHU
Rutgers > Seton Hall in every way you look at it unless you are playing baseball or basketball for the school

key_points: Cost vs. Value: $100K debt for marketing degree with no strong ROI argument
Weekend Life: Campus described as effectively dead with inactive clubs
Fire Safety Legacy: Boland Hall arson in 2000 killed 3 students; culture of trauma still shapes campus
key_quotes: The Seton Hall in the brochures and the tours was nothing like the reality with nothing to do on campus on the weekends
Many clubs are listed but are inactive. If you can find a club, many clubs met during class time
3 dead, 5 severely injured, 51 normal injured

key_points: Accounting program has a real pipeline to Big Four firms in NYC
Administration showed flexibility on refunding a $750 deposit when pushed
Seton Hall's Diplomacy and International Relations graduate program has a strong reputation
key_quotes: I am also pursuing accounting at Seton Hall. Congratulations! Really happy for you
I EMAILED THEM AND IM GETTING MY DEPOSIT REFUNDED
I wish to be there to attend the Master's degree later PhD, in Politics concretely in Diplomacy and International Relations
